items.passage: Wei Ming missed the school bus. He ran all the way to school and arrived just in time for assembly.
items.stem: Why did Wei Ming run to school?
items.rationale: The passage says he missed the bus, so he ran to get there in time.

items.passage: Siti helped her grandmother carry the groceries up to their HDB flat. Her grandmother smiled and thanked her.
items.stem: How did the grandmother feel?
items.rationale: She smiled and thanked Siti, which shows she felt grateful.

items.passage: The sky turned grey and the wind grew strong. Kavitha quickly took the washing off the line before the rain came.
items.stem: Why did Kavitha take the washing in quickly?
items.rationale: The grey sky and strong wind are signs that rain was coming.

items.passage: At the hawker centre, Mr Tan ordered chicken rice and a cup of kopi. He paid three dollars.
items.stem: What did Mr Tan drink?
items.rationale: The passage says he ordered a cup of kopi.

items.passage: The puppy hid under the table and shivered when it heard the loud thunder.
items.stem: How did the puppy feel?
items.rationale: Hiding and shivering at the thunder show the puppy was frightened.

items.passage: Aisha practised the piano every day after school. At the concert, she played without a single mistake.
items.stem: Why did Aisha play so well at the concert?
items.rationale: Her daily practice is the reason she played without mistakes.

items.passage: The class went to the Science Centre. They saw a show about the stars and planets.
items.stem: Where did the class go?
items.rationale: The passage says the class went to the Science Centre.

items.passage: Ravi found a wallet on the MRT. He handed it to the station staff instead of keeping it.
items.stem: What does this tell us about Ravi?
items.rationale: Returning the wallet instead of keeping it shows he is honest.

items.passage: It was the last day of the school holidays. Meiling packed her bag and laid out her uniform for the morning.
items.stem: What was Meiling getting ready for?
items.rationale: Packing her bag and uniform on the last day of the holidays shows school was starting.

items.passage: The market was almost empty. Most of the stallholders had already sold out and were packing up for the day.
items.stem: Why was the market almost empty?
items.rationale: Stallholders selling out and packing up show it was near the end of the day.

items.passage: Meiling planted three sunflower seeds in a pot. Every day she watered them and put the pot near the window.
items.stem: What did Meiling grow?
items.rationale: The passage says she planted sunflower seeds.

items.passage: Ravi's stomach growled loudly during the last lesson. He kept looking at the clock, waiting for the recess bell.
items.stem: How did Ravi most likely feel?
items.rationale: A growling stomach and waiting for recess show he was hungry.

items.passage: The National Day Parade was held at the Padang. Fireworks lit up the night sky.
items.stem: Where was the parade held?
items.rationale: The passage says the parade was held at the Padang.

items.passage: Siti wrapped the present carefully and hid it under her bed. Tomorrow was her mother's birthday.
items.stem: Who was the present for?
items.rationale: Tomorrow was her mother's birthday, so the present was for her mother.

items.passage: The players hugged one another and cheered as the final whistle blew.
items.stem: What most likely happened?
items.rationale: Hugging and cheering at the final whistle show their team won.

items.passage: Grandpa reads the newspaper every morning while drinking his kopi.
items.stem: What does Grandpa drink in the morning?
items.rationale: The passage says he drinks his kopi.

items.passage: Aisha's eyes were red and she kept sniffing as she read the last page of the sad story.
items.stem: How did the story make Aisha feel?
items.rationale: Red eyes and sniffing over a sad story show she felt sad.

items.passage: The floor was wet and a yellow sign stood nearby. Mr Lim walked slowly and carefully past it.
items.stem: Why did Mr Lim walk carefully?
items.rationale: A wet floor and warning sign mean it was slippery, so he walked carefully.

items.passage: At the library, Kavitha borrowed two storybooks and one atlas.
items.stem: How many storybooks did Kavitha borrow?
items.rationale: The passage says she borrowed two storybooks.

items.passage: Wei Ming shared his umbrella with a classmate who had none, even though he got a little wet himself.
items.stem: What kind of boy is Wei Ming?
items.rationale: Sharing his umbrella and getting wet himself shows he is kind.

items.passage: The lights suddenly went out and everyone in the flat reached for their torches.
items.stem: What had most likely happened?
items.rationale: Lights going out and everyone reaching for torches point to a power cut.

items.passage: Nurul scored the winning goal in the netball match.
items.stem: What sport did Nurul play?
items.rationale: The passage says she played in the netball match.

items.passage: The queue outside the new bubble tea shop stretched all the way around the corner.
items.stem: What does the long queue suggest?
items.rationale: A very long queue suggests many people wanted to buy from it.

items.passage: Ahmad checked his answers twice before handing in his paper and walking out with a smile.
items.stem: How did Ahmad feel about the test?
items.rationale: Checking his answers and smiling as he left show he felt confident.

CM-comprehension.iCanStatement: I can find and work out answers from a passage.
CM-comprehension.explanation: Some answers are stated in the passage — read carefully to find them. Others you must work out from clues (how someone acts or feels). Always check your answer against what the passage actually says.

workedExamples.note: Clue words tell you she felt afraid, even if the passage never says 'afraid'.
What a person does shows what they are like — here, honest.
